channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple’s iPhone sales broke a new quarterly record during the holidays, despite AI blunders
item.description: Apple’s iPhone sales soared to a new quarterly record during the holiday season, despite artificial intelligence blunders that prompted the technology trendsetter to get a helping hand from Google.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Resilience is essential as demands on government networks multiply
item.description: Agencies can’t predict the next attack, new mandates or the next cloud outage. But they can control the strength of their network foundation.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Startup Amutable plotting Linux security overhaul to counter hacking threats
item.description: If there’s one thing guaranteed to grab attention in the computer security world, it’s announcing yourself without fully explaining what it is you plan to do.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Founding Fathers with extra bounce: New bobblehead set marks America’s 250th
item.description: "There's a lot of people who are really interested in the nation's history and bobbleheads are a perfect way for that," Phil Sklar said.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: This whole AI thing is simpler than you think
item.description: At least three-quarters of the speaking invitations I get these days are about AI. But lately, they’re for different reasons. Companies used to bring me in under the assumption that artificial intelligence was going to change everything.
item.dc:creator: Douglas Rushkoff, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX said to consider merger with Tesla or xAI
item.description: SpaceX is considering a potential merger with Tesla Inc., as well as an alternative combination with artificial intelligence firm xAI, according to people familiar with the matter, a sign billionaire Elon Musk is weighing how to consolidate his empire.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Reid Hoffman says business leaders must speak up
item.description: As global leaders disperse from the World Economic Forum, LinkedIn cofounder and tech investor Reid Hoffman breaks down the biggest challenges and opportunities facing business today—from political headwinds tied to immigration and geopolitics to why fears of a tech bubble aren’t shaping his investi
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: How hackers are turning the power grid and digital infrastructure into a weapon
item.description: The darkness that swept over the Venezuelan capital in the predawn hours of Jan. 3, 2026, signaled a profound shift in the nature of modern conflict: the convergence of physical and cyber warfare. While U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Top 11 network outages and application failures of 2025
item.description: System outages plagued organizations of all sizes in 2025, with major incidents exposing vulnerabilities across network infrastructure and the IT dependencies that define today’s distributed environments, according to the latest Internet outage report from Cisco ThousandEyes.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple's iPhone sales surge to new quarterly high despite early missteps in artificial intelligence
item.description: Apple’s iPhone sales soared to a new quarterly record during the holiday season, despite artificial intelligence blunders that prompted the technology trendsetter to get a helping hand from Google.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Amazon in talks to invest up to $50 billion in OpenAI, WSJ says
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. is in talks to invest as much as $50 billion in OpenAI, the Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday, a deal that would accelerate ties between the world's largest cloud-computing provider and the leading builder of AI tools.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Suse offers cloud sovereignty assessment tool
item.description: Suse has unveiled a Cloud Sovereignty Framework Self Assessment tool, with the intention of helping customers understand the gaps in their compliance with the 2025 EU Cloud Sovereignty Framework.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft touts M365 Copilot momentum, claims 15M paid users
item.description: Microsoft has for the first time reported Microsoft 365 Copilot adoption stats, boasting this week of 15 million paid seats (individual user licenses).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why a lack of governance will hurt companies using agentic AI
item.description: Businesses are acting fast to adopt agentic AI—artificial intelligence systems that work without human guidance—but have been much slower to put governance in place to oversee them, a new survey shows. That mismatch is a major source of risk in AI adoption.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Thousands of iPhone apps expose data inside Apple App Store
item.description: Researchers found Apple iOS app security flaws that expose user data and payment systems despite positive App Store reviews, according to Cybernews.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What is prompt engineering? The art of AI orchestration
item.description: Prompt engineering is the process of crafting inputs, or prompts, to a generative AI system that lead to the system producing better outputs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Big Tech CEOs’ tortured statements on ICE in Minnesota show an industry captured by Trump
item.description: On the morning of January 24 in downtown Minneapolis, an ICE agent shot and killed protester Alex Pretti, an ICU nurse at a local Veterans Affairs hospital. Just 2 miles away, on January 7, another ICE agent had shot and killed Nicole Renee Good, a mother.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nuclear safety rules quietly rewritten to favor AI
item.description: The US Department of Energy (DoE) program that would see three new experimental commercial nuclear reactors launched by July 4 is the driver behind the secret rewriting of nuclear safety and security standards, says a report released Wednesday by NPR.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Crooks are hijacking and reselling AI infrastructure: Report
item.description: For years, CSOs have worried about their IT infrastructure being used for unauthorized cryptomining. Now, say researchers, they’d better start worrying about crooks hijacking and reselling access to exposed corporate AI infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Crooks are hijacking and reselling AI infrastructure: Report
item.description: For years, CSOs have worried about their IT infrastructure being used for unauthorized cryptomining. Now, say researchers, they’d better start worrying about crooks hijacking and reselling access to exposed corporate AI infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google’s LiteRT adds advanced hardware acceleration
item.description: LiteRT, Google’s “modern” on-device inference framework evolved from TensorFlow Lite (TFLite), has introduced advanced acceleration capabilities, based on a ”next-generation GPU engine” called ML Drift.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: WhatsApp gets new security features
item.description: The popular messaging app WhatsApp got a range of new security features Tuesday with the launch of “Strict Account Settings.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Amazon confirms 16,000 job cuts, including to AWS
item.description: Amazon is cutting about 16,000 jobs across the company, SVP of People Experience and Technology Beth Galetti wrote in an email to employees Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenSilver 3.3 runs Blazor components inside XAML apps
item.description: Userware has released OpenSilver 3.3, an update to the open-source framework for building cross-platform applications using C# and XAML. OpenSilver 3.3 lets Blazor components for web development run directly inside XAML applications, streamlining the process of running these components.
item.dc:creator: Info World
